tier1_summary.activity_description: Filter inquiries, translate needs into systems engineering operands.
tier1_summary.quality_control_point: Verify notification contains readable technical data.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Wasting engineering time on out-of-scope inquiries.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Evaluate institutional capability, hardware, and specific project risks.
tier1_summary.quality_control_point: Ensure workshop tools can reach required tolerances.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Accepting a project that exceeds machine precision limits.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Develop detailed SOW and multi-layer cost estimation for the project brief.
tier1_summary.quality_control_point: Verify markup layers cover regional supply chain volatility.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Underestimating direct costs for imported components.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Map technical variables into legal clauses and secure executive risk sign-off.
tier1_summary.quality_control_point: Confirm 'Background IP' is protected in Section 6.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Client assuming full ownership of IMHO background IP.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Verify advance payment clearance and authorize engineering to commence.
tier1_summary.quality_control_point: Cross-reference payment with milestone trigger D3.3.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Beginning engineering work without cleared liquidity.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Formally assign team roster and freeze the project schedule/budget baseline.
tier1_summary.quality_control_point: Verify assigned engineer has correct CAD license.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Assigning personnel who are already 100% utilized.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Execute functional synthesis, morphological matrices, and 3D layout construction.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Designing a system that is functional but unmanufacturable.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Decompose the master model into production drawings with standard GD&T.
tier1_summary.quality_control_point: Verify view arrangements are logical and clear.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Missing dimensions leading to fabrication errors.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Extract BOM from locked CAD and manage vendor selection/receiving.
tier1_summary.quality_control_point: Verify incoming goods match technical spec (D7.6).
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Delayed lead times for long-lead COTS components.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Execute physical build following work orders and routing travelers.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Welding distortion rendering parts out of tolerance.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Perform dimensional audits and dynamic load/function validation.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Releasing a machine with undetected structural defects.

tier1_summary.activity_description: Lock native CAD, compile TDP, and transfer liability to client.
tier1_summary.quality_control_point: Verify Final Handover Certificate (D11.4) is signed.
tier1_summary.risk_considerations: Maintaining liability for machines after physical delivery.

tier3_summary.activity_description: Capture physical dimensions and source asset quality to ensure CAD accuracy.
tier3_summary.risk_considerations: Inaccurate field data leads to unusable CAD models.

tier3_summary.activity_description: Generate a combined scope and price offer to bypass heavy feasibility stages.
tier3_summary.quality_control_point: Ensure IP Buyout fee is discussed if native files are requested.

tier3_summary.activity_description: Define IP ownership (Native vs PDF) and waive liability for client-provided data errors.
tier3_summary.quality_control_point: Confirm 'Liability Waiver' is signed for client-provided models.
tier3_summary.risk_considerations: Legal disputes over ownership of source CAD files.

tier3_summary.activity_description: Verify final payment in Fidelity Bank before authorizing digital file delivery.
tier3_summary.quality_control_point: Cross-reference IP Buyout payment with contract terms.
tier3_summary.risk_considerations: Releasing native CAD before final GHS clearance.

tier3_summary.activity_description: Assign drafting tasks directly to a CAD engineer, bypassing full capacity reviews.
tier3_summary.quality_control_point: Confirm assigned engineer has correct software license.
tier3_summary.risk_considerations: Assigning an engineer without specific 'Surfacing' or 'Reverse Eng' skills.

tier3_summary.activity_description: Execute the 3D construction from field measurements and validate against design intent.
tier3_summary.quality_control_point: Validate 'As-Built' vs 'Corrected' features with Lead Eng.
tier3_summary.risk_considerations: Modeling 'wear and tear' instead of functional design intent.

tier3_summary.activity_description: Secure client digital sign-off and formally archive the technical data package.
tier3_summary.quality_control_point: Verify 'Finance Unblock' is active before emailing final assets.
tier3_summary.risk_considerations: Sending high-res assets to client before formal CAD Lock.

activities.activity_description: Receive automatic notification of a new client inquiry via website form or email.
documents_generated: Verify the notification contains readable text and contact info.

activities.activity_description: Read the inquiry to perform a preliminary spam/relevance filter.
documents_generated: Ensure the request broadly aligns with IMHOGEN's engineering services.

activities.activity_description: Create a new project entry in the tracker and copy/paste all client details into the respective fields.
documents_generated: Double-check that phone numbers and email addresses are copied without typos.

activities.activity_description: Send standard acknowledgment email to the client stating the inquiry was received and outlining the next step.
documents_generated: Verify the client's name is spelled correctly in the template.

activities.activity_description: Contact the client to propose 2-3 available time slots for a technical discovery meeting.
documents_generated: Ensure proposed times fall within standard operating hours.

activities.activity_description: Generate a calendar invite with a video conferencing link and a standard meeting agenda, and send it to the client.
documents_generated: Verify the video link works and the time zone is correct.

activities.activity_description: Conduct the discovery meeting to extract exact technical needs, operational constraints, and expected outcomes.
documents_generated: Ensure the client explicitly states their budget constraints and timelines.

activities.activity_description: Clean up raw meeting notes and upload them into the central documentation repository.
documents_generated: Ensure all technical jargon and constraints are accurately transcribed.

activities.activity_description: Share the Needs Assessment Notes with the Engineering and R&D team for internal review.
documents_generated: Confirm receipt of the document by the Engineering team.

activities.activity_description: Evaluate if the department has the required CAD software, tooling, and skills to execute the client's request.
documents_generated: Cross-check required software (e.g.
Inventor) against current licenses.

activities.activity_description: Evaluate the request for regulatory, safety, and operational risks.
documents_generated: Ensure all physical safety standards related to the request are flagged.

activities.activity_description: Compile the Capability and Risk assessments into a final Go/No-Go Feasibility Report.
documents_generated: Final managerial review of the Go
No-Go recommendation.

activities.activity_description: If "Go", write out the exact technical deliverables (CAD models, BOMs, reports) the department will provide.
documents_generated: Ensure deliverables are strictly defined to prevent scope creep.

activities.activity_description: Estimate the required engineering man-hours, software usage time, and any specialized resource costs.
documents_generated: Verify hourly rates match the current department standards.

activities.activity_description: Combine the Scope of Work and Cost Estimation into the formal Project Brief & Draft Budget document.
documents_generated: Check formatting
spelling
and mathematical accuracy of the budget.

activities.activity_description: Conduct a brief internal meeting to formally hand over the Project Brief to the contract administration team.
documents_generated: Both parties must verbally agree that the scope is clear enough for contracting.

activities.activity_description: Change the project status in the central database from "Onboarding" to "Contracting Phase".
documents_generated: Verify the status change is reflected across all dashboards.

activities.activity_description: Archive all onboarding raw documents (emails, initial notes) into the project's permanent digital folder.
documents_generated: Ensure all files are correctly named and tagged before archiving.

activities.activity_description: Receive the formal Project Brief & Draft Budget from the Onboarding handoff and review for clarity.
documents_generated: Ensure all technical deliverables and budget figures are explicitly stated.

activities.activity_description: Access the central legal repository and download the appropriate standard contract templates (SLA, NDA, SOW).
documents_generated: Verify the templates are the most currently approved versions by legal.

activities.activity_description: Populate the selected templates with client details, scope of work, budget, and specific constraints.
documents_generated: Double-check client legal name
addresses
and monetary figures.

activities.activity_description: Route the Initial Draft Contract to the Project Manager to verify that the technical scope matches the department's capability.
documents_generated: PM must sign off that the timeline and technical deliverables are realistic.

activities.activity_description: Route the PM Reviewed Draft to the Finance Team to verify payment milestones, taxes, and total contract value.
documents_generated: Finance must verify the payment schedule aligns with cash flow needs.

activities.activity_description: Submit the draft to the Legal Advisor (if applicable) for a final compliance and liability check.
documents_generated: Check for excessive liability
indemnification
or IP forfeiture clauses.

activities.activity_description: Draft a formal email and send the Internally Approved Draft to the client for their review.
documents_generated: Ensure the email clearly states the deadline for their review
redlines.

activities.activity_description: Receive client feedback/redlines. If changes are requested, schedule a negotiation meeting.
documents_generated: Ensure relevant decision-makers (Head of Ops
PM) are invited.

activities.activity_description: Conduct the negotiation meeting, discuss disputed clauses, and agree on mutual compromises.
documents_generated: Never agree to technical or financial changes without PM
Finance input.

activities.activity_description: Update the contract document based on the agreed compromises and conduct a final internal read-through.
documents_generated: Ensure no unintended clauses were altered during the redline process.

activities.activity_description: Upload the Finalized Contract into the digital signature platform and tag the required signature fields.
documents_generated: Verify the correct authorized signers for both IMHOGEN and the client.

activities.activity_description: Dispatch the digital envelope to the client and monitor the platform for completion.
documents_generated: Send automatic 48-hour reminders if the document remains unsigned.

activities.activity_description: Once signed by all parties, download the final document and its digital audit trail certificate.
documents_generated: Verify the audit certificate is attached to prove legal validity.

activities.activity_description: Upload the Executed Contract into the central secure legal repository and apply appropriate metadata tags.
documents_generated: Ensure strict access control (read-only) is applied to the final file.

activities.activity_description: Update the CRM/Project Tracker status to "Contract Executed" and trigger a notification to the Finance Team to begin Payment Scheduling.
documents_generated: Confirm the Finance Team acknowledges the trigger to start billing.

detailed_framework.name: Payment Scheduling and Fund Management Process.

activities.activity_description: Receive the "Phase Initiation Notice" and the Executed Contract from the Business Operations Team.
documents_generated: Confirm receipt of the fully executed contract before initiating any financial setup.

activities.activity_description: Read the contract's commercial terms to extract exact milestone descriptions, payment percentages, and due dates.
documents_generated: Verify that the extracted milestones exactly match the contract's language.

activities.activity_description: Create a new client profile and project ledger in the accounting system, inputting the extracted payment schedule.
documents_generated: Finance Manager must perform a spot-check on the entered schedule vs the contract.

activities.activity_description: Receive formal notification and proof of completion for a specific project milestone from the Project Manager.
documents_generated: Verify the PM's proof of completion aligns with the contract's definition of the milestone.

activities.activity_description: Generate a draft invoice in the accounting software corresponding to the achieved milestone and scheduled amount.
documents_generated: Ensure PO numbers
tax IDs
and bank details are clearly visible on the invoice.

activities.activity_description: Review and approve the draft invoice for mathematical accuracy and tax compliance before issuance.
documents_generated: Dual-authorization required for invoices exceeding a predefined monetary threshold.

activities.activity_description: Draft a formal email, attach the Approved Invoice (PDF format), and transmit it to the client's designated billing contact.
documents_generated: Verify the email address is the official accounts payable contact
not just the technical lead.

activities.activity_description: Log into the corporate banking portal daily to monitor and download reports of incoming client deposits.
documents_generated: Ensure secure network connections and VPNs are used when accessing banking portals.

activities.activity_description: Cross-reference incoming bank deposits against outstanding invoices in the accounting software.
documents_generated: Match the exact deposited amount and payment reference number to the specific invoice.

activities.activity_description: Generate a formal Payment Receipt and email it to the client to acknowledge successful receipt of funds.
documents_generated: Issue the receipt within 24 hours of the funds clearing the bank.

activities.activity_description: Change the invoice status to "Paid" and send a "Payment Cleared - Proceed" notification to the Project Manager.
documents_generated: PM must acknowledge the unblock notice before deploying engineering resources.

activities.activity_description: Generate an Accounts Receivable (AR) aging report weekly to identify invoices that have crossed their payment grace period.
documents_generated: Ensure the report accurately reflects the net terms (e.g.
Net 30) agreed in the contract.

activities.activity_description: Send a polite, standardized follow-up email to clients with overdue invoices, requesting payment status.
documents_generated: Maintain a professional tone to preserve the client relationship while being firm on terms.

activities.activity_description: If payment is delayed beyond critical thresholds, issue a formal "Stop Work" notice to the Project Manager.
documents_generated: Escalate to Head of Business Operations before halting major engineering work.

detailed_framework.name: Team Assignment and Capacity Planning Process.

activities.activity_description: Receive the "Phase Initiation Notice" and the finalized Project Brief from the Business Operations Team.
documents_generated: Verify the Project Brief contains explicitly approved timelines and budgets.

activities.activity_description: Read the Project Brief to list the specific technical competencies, software platforms (e.g., Inventor), and estimated man-hours required.
documents_generated: Ensure all required niche skills or specialized software modules are identified.

activities.activity_description: Populate the standard Resource Request Form with the required roles, hours, and duration, and submit it to HR/Engineering Management.
documents_generated: Double-check that the requested man-hours align with the approved budget.

activities.activity_description: Receive the Resource Request Form and open the department's scheduling dashboard to view current staff workloads.
documents_generated: Ensure no single engineer is assigned a sustained workload exceeding 85% capacity.

activities.activity_description: Cross-reference the requested competencies with the skills matrix of available engineers, draftsmen, and QA/QC staff.
documents_generated: Match the complexity of the project with the seniority level of the assigned engineer.

activities.activity_description: Select the specific personnel for the project and formally log their assigned hours and roles into the tracking system.
documents_generated: Verify that planned leave or PTO does not conflict with critical project milestones.

activities.activity_description: Receive the Team Assignment Record and trigger the IT/Admin provisioning workflow.
documents_generated: Ensure only the individuals listed on the Assignment Record receive access.

activities.activity_description: Create dedicated project folders in the central Document Management System and CAD PDM Vault, applying strict access controls.
documents_generated: Apply standardized naming conventions to all newly created digital folders.

activities.activity_description: Set up dedicated internal communication channels (e.g., specific chat groups or email threads) and invite the assigned team.
documents_generated: Ensure the Project Manager is granted "Admin" rights over the new communication channels.

activities.activity_description: Draft a structured kick-off presentation summarizing the client's goals, the technical constraints, the budget, and the timeline.
documents_generated: Ensure all proprietary client details are masked if the presentation is stored broadly.

activities.activity_description: Send a calendar invite for the internal kick-off meeting to the assigned team and attach the Project Brief for pre-reading.
documents_generated: Verify that the scheduled time works for all assigned critical personnel.

activities.activity_description: Conduct the video/in-person kick-off meeting, explicitly outlining the roles, expectations, and the "black-box" input/output deliverables.
documents_generated: Require verbal confirmation from the Lead Engineer that the timeline is understood.

activities.activity_description: Log into the Project Tracking Software, formally lock the baseline schedule/budget, and change the project status to "Engineering Design Phase".
documents_generated: Ensure the baseline matches the kick-off agreements exactly; restrict editing access.

detailed_framework.name: Standard Engineering Design Process (P1 - P6 Framework)

activities.activity_description: Establish rough factors for all life phases (technology, operators, inputs, outputs) and analyze the TP environment.
documents_generated: Verify all input
output operands match the Project Brief constraints.

activities.activity_description: Quantify requirements, assign tolerances, and allocate properties across functional life phases and operators.
documents_generated: Validate tolerances against available standard workshop capabilities.

activities.activity_description: Compile, review, and lock the formal, multi-dimensional design specifications.
documents_generated: Formal sign-off on the Pr-Set before any design work begins.

activities.activity_description: Analyze the technical process viewpoints, assess difficulty, and assign specific design tasks to operators.
documents_generated: Ensure assigned engineers have the required technical competency.

activities.activity_description: Estimate design costs, map the schedule, and set up phase progress tracking mechanisms.
documents_generated: Baseline schedule must align with overall project budget and timeline.

activities.activity_description: Establish the operands (Material, Energy, Information) and the technological principles of the operations process.
documents_generated: Verify logical flow: all input operands must logically convert to outputs.

activities.activity_description: Distribute effects between technical/human systems, defining main, assisting, and evoked functions.
documents_generated: Ensure no critical load-bearing or safety function is overlooked.

activities.activity_description: Populate the morphological matrix with action principles and combine function carriers (organs) into unified concepts.
documents_generated: Ensure at least 3 distinct conceptual variants are generated.

activities.activity_description: Establish spatial topologies (Iconic/Principle) and evaluate proposals to select the optimal Final Organ Structure.
documents_generated: Justify the selected organ structure mathematically against the Pr-Set.

activities.activity_description: Establish preliminary layouts, rough forms, and define constructional design groups from the organ structure.
documents_generated: Clash detection and clearance check at the preliminary layout stage.

activities.activity_description: Definitively establish dimensions, material treatments, part decompositions, and perform strength analyses.
documents_generated: Tolerance stack-up analysis and FEA validation against the Pr-Set.

activities.activity_description: Generate individual detailed manufacturing drawings, control assembly drawings, and hierarchical part lists.
documents_generated: Drawing checker review (Title blocks
GD&T
notes) by Lead Engineer.

activities.activity_description: Prepare manufacturing instructions, conduct a final design audit, and release the complete technical data package.
documents_generated: Complete sign-off matrix verification prior to manufacturing release.

activities.activity_description: Receive the "Approved Technical Data Package" (CAD, Drawings, BOM, Reports) from the Lead Engineer.
documents_generated: Verify all files possess the required internal QA
QC signature before sending to the client.

activities.activity_description: Upload the deliverables into a secure, client-facing digital portal or package them into a secure file-transfer link.
documents_generated: Ensure access permissions are strictly limited to the authorized client contacts.

activities.activity_description: Draft and send a calendar invite for a formal "Deliverable Presentation Meeting" to walk the client through the design.
documents_generated: Schedule the meeting within 48 hours of sending the deliverables to maintain momentum.

activities.activity_description: Conduct the presentation meeting, using 3D viewers to explain technical decisions, constraints, and functional compliance.
documents_generated: Ensure the Lead Engineer explains why certain design constraints were enforced.

activities.activity_description: Send the standardized "Client Feedback Form" to the client immediately following the presentation.
documents_generated: Formally state the deadline (e.g.
5 business days) for submitting the feedback form.

activities.activity_description: Receive the completed feedback form from the client and extract all requested changes into the central Revision Log.
documents_generated: Ensure every single client comment is captured line-by-line without summarization.

activities.activity_description: Review the Master Revision Log against the original signed Contract/Project Brief to classify changes as "Minor" or "Major".
documents_generated: "Major" changes (out of scope) must immediately trigger a financial Change Order request.

activities.activity_description: Route approved "Minor" revisions back to the engineering team, assigning specific PID tasks for CAD/Drawing updates.
documents_generated: Ensure the revision tickets explicitly link back to the specific client feedback line item.

activities.activity_description: Execute the requested changes in the CAD models and update the associated 2D drawings and BOMs.
documents_generated: Save the revised files as a new version (e.g.
V1.1) to preserve the original baseline.

activities.activity_description: Perform a QA/QC check on the revised files to ensure the specific feedback was addressed without breaking other system functions.
activities.tools: Revised Technical Data Package, Revision Log
documents_generated: Verify that the new changes still comply with the original Pr-Set safety and load requirements.

activities.activity_description: Generate the formal "Delivery & Acceptance Form" stating that all design requirements and revisions have been met.
documents_generated: Cross-check the final deliverable list on the form against the original contract.

activities.activity_description: Upload the Acceptance Form and the Final Revised Technical Data Package into the e-signature platform.
documents_generated: Verify the digital envelope is sent to the client's authorized legal
financial signatory.

activities.activity_description: Conduct a brief final Handover Meeting (if requested by the client) to officially close the design phase and answer final questions.
documents_generated: Confirm verbally during the meeting that no further engineering changes will be made.

activities.activity_description: Receive the executed e-signature, archive all final documents, and trigger the Finance Team to issue the final invoice.
documents_generated: Ensure all finalized CAD models are locked in the PDM vault to prevent further editing.

activities.activity_description: Receive the "Project Phase Closure Notice" and the finalized, QA-approved Bill of Materials (BOM) from the Project Manager.
documents_generated: Verify the BOM has the formal "Released for Manufacturing" digital signature.

activities.activity_description: Import the digital BOM into the procurement dashboard and categorize items into raw materials, standard hardware, and custom components.
documents_generated: Ensure units of measure (e.g.
mm
meters
kg) are correctly mapped during import.

activities.activity_description: Cross-reference the Categorized Procurement List against the locked financial budget established in the Project Brief.
documents_generated: Flag any item where the current market price exceeds the estimated baseline budget by >10%.

activities.activity_description: Generate a Request for Quotation (RFQ) for required materials, specifying exact tolerances, grades, and quantities.
documents_generated: Ensure RFQs for standard structural steel clearly state acceptable tolerance limits for local market variations.

activities.activity_description: Dispatch RFQs to approved local suppliers (e.g., Suame/Kumasi market vendors) and specialized international distributors via email.
documents_generated: Verify vendor email addresses and set a strict deadline (e.g.
48 hours) for quote returns.

activities.activity_description: Receive quotes, compile a comparative vendor matrix (price, lead time, quality history), and select the optimal suppliers.
documents_generated: Justify selection if the cheapest vendor is not chosen (e.g.
due to faster lead time or better quality).

activities.activity_description: Generate formal Purchase Orders (POs) in the accounting system for the selected vendors, attaching the required engineering specs.
documents_generated: Double-check payment terms
delivery address
and required delivery dates on the PO.

activities.activity_description: Route the Draft PO to the Finance Manager for final financial authorization and fund allocation.
documents_generated: Finance Manager must verify that sufficient cash flow exists to clear the PO upon delivery.

activities.activity_description: Transmit the Approved PO to the vendor and obtain a formal acknowledgment and confirmed delivery schedule.
documents_generated: Do not mark the item as "Ordered" until the vendor explicitly confirms receipt and capacity.

activities.activity_description: Receive the physical delivery at the workshop. Cross-reference the vendor's packing slip against the original PO.
documents_generated: Verify physical box counts and part numbers before the delivery driver leaves the premises.

activities.activity_description: Perform incoming Quality Control (QC) on the raw materials using measuring tools (calipers, micrometers) to verify dimensions against the BOM.
activities.tools: Final CAD BOM, Initial Receiving Log
documents_generated: Mandate a dimensional and visual check for warping
rust
or incorrect thickness on structural steel.

activities.activity_description: If materials pass QC, formally log them into the digital inventory system and assign them to the specific project storage zone.
documents_generated: Apply physical project tags
labels to the materials to prevent them from being used on other jobs.

activities.activity_description: Trigger a notification to the Fabrication Supervisor that all required materials are in stock, QA-approved, and ready for production.
documents_generated: Supervisor must acknowledge the notice to trigger the Fabrication timeline.

activities.activity_description: Receive the "Fabrication Readiness Notice", Final BOM, and 2D Engineering Drawings from Procurement and Engineering.
documents_generated: Ensure all 2D drawings have the correct "Released for Manufacturing" stamp and GD&T notes.

activities.activity_description: Review the 2D drawings and BOM to sequence the manufacturing operations (e.g., rough cut → machine → form → join → finish).
documents_generated: Verify that the sequence logically prevents impossible tool-access or assembly situations later in the build.

activities.activity_description: Identify, reserve, and calibrate all required workshop machinery, CNC centers, fixtures, and hand tools needed for the job.
documents_generated: QA
QC must verify that measuring tapes
calipers
and gauges are properly calibrated.

activities.activity_description: Conduct a mandatory "Toolbox Talk" to brief the shop floor team on the exact structural goals, GD&T tolerances, and specific process hazards.
documents_generated: Require machine operators and fabricators to verbally confirm their understanding of the critical datum planes.

activities.activity_description: Issue the necessary Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) tailored to the specific fabrication processes (e.g., eye protection, respirators, hearing protection).
documents_generated: Deny workshop floor access to any operator lacking complete
functional PPE.

activities.activity_description: Perform initial shaping, cutting, or blanking of raw materials to the exact preliminary dimensions specified in the BOM/Cut List.
documents_generated: Verify the dimensions of the first piece before processing the rest of the batch (First-Article Inspection).

activities.activity_description: Deburr, chamfer, and clean the edges/surfaces of all prepped materials to prepare them for safe handling and high-integrity joining or final machining.
documents_generated: Visually inspect surfaces to ensure no burrs
oil
or scale remains that could interfere with alignment.

activities.activity_description: Load the prepped materials into the designated fixtures, jigs, or machine beds, aligning them strictly to the primary datum planes.
documents_generated: Ensure all locating pins and clamps are fully seated before initiating the primary manufacturing process.

activities.activity_description: Execute preliminary forming, temporary fastening, or rough machining passes to establish the baseline geometry.
documents_generated: QA
QC must perform a quick squareness and dimensional check before final
irreversible joining
machining begins.

activities.activity_description: Execute the final manufacturing sequence (e.g., final joining, structural fastening, or finishing machine passes) utilizing methods to evenly distribute stress and prevent distortion.
documents_generated: Strictly enforce the defined process sequence to mitigate mechanical or thermal distortion across the structure.

activities.activity_description: Perform visual, dimensional, and (if required) Non-Destructive Testing (NDT) on the structural joints and critical machined surfaces.
documents_generated: Flag any defective joint or out-of-tolerance surface for immediate rework before moving to the next stage.

activities.activity_description: Execute final finishing operations (e.g., grinding flush, polishing, edge routing) to meet the aesthetic and functional surface requirements.
documents_generated: Ensure finishing operations do not reduce material thickness or alter dimensions below the safe minimum limits.

activities.activity_description: Apply standard environmental protections (e.g., primer, paint, anodizing, or anti-rust oil) as dictated by the engineering specifications.
documents_generated: Ensure coatings are applied evenly and do not obstruct any threaded holes
sliding rails
or moving joints.

activities.activity_description: Move the Coated Final Product to the designated testing staging area and update the project tracker to "Ready for Final QA Testing".
documents_generated: Supervisor must digitally sign off that all fabrication steps on the Job Process Sheet were completed.

activities.activity_description: Receive the "Fabrication Completion Notice" and physically transfer the fabricated structure to the QA/QC staging area.
documents_generated: Ensure the structure is securely staged and safe to inspect before beginning work.

activities.activity_description: Retrieve the locked Design Specification (Pr-Set), 2D Drawings, and the original Project Brief from the digital vault.
documents_generated: Verify that the retrieved drawings are the exact versions used by the fabrication team.

activities.activity_description: Perform a comprehensive visual sweep of the structure, checking for sharp edges, burrs, spatter, and coating uniformity.
documents_generated: Flag any exposed
uncoated raw steel that violates the environmental protection specs.

activities.activity_description: Measure all critical datums, hole centers, and overall bounding dimensions against the defined GD&T tolerances (e.g., ±1.0mm).
documents_generated: Ensure measuring tools are zeroed and calibrated before taking the first measurement.

activities.activity_description: Prepare the structure for physical testing by securely anchoring it and gathering test operands (e.g., inserting sample pipes into the welding jig).
documents_generated: Ensure all anchors are tight before applying any dynamic or static loads.

activities.activity_description: Actuate all moving parts, clamps, and mechanisms to verify smooth operation, clamping force, and structural deflection under load (e.g., ≤ 0.5 mm).
documents_generated: Verify that the structure returns to absolute zero (no permanent deformation) after the load is removed.

activities.activity_description: If any dimension or test fails, immediately tag the physical structure with a red "DO NOT USE" marker and log the defect.
documents_generated: The NCR must cite the specific drawing coordinate or Pr-Set line item that failed.

activities.activity_description: Route the NCR back to the Fabrication Supervisor and Lead Engineer to determine if the part must be reworked, repaired, or scrapped.
documents_generated: Do not authorize a "repair" if the structural integrity has been fundamentally compromised.

activities.activity_description: Once all tests pass (or rework is successfully re-tested), compile all inspection logs into the final validation package.
documents_generated: The Lead Engineer must countersign the QA Certificate to accept ultimate technical liability.

activities.activity_description: Attach the physical QA Release Certificate to the structure and update the digital project status to "Ready for Delivery/Handover".
documents_generated: Ensure the Finance Team is copied on the notice to prepare the final invoice.

activities.activity_description: Receive the "Final QA Release Certificate" and interview the Fabrication Supervisor for any on-the-fly workshop modifications made during the build.
documents_generated: Ensure every authorized rework or dimensional shift is captured from the QA reports.

activities.activity_description: Update the 3D CAD models, 2D Drawings, and BOM to reflect the exact "As-Built" physical reality of the approved structure.
documents_generated: Increment the file revision number (e.g.
Rev A to Rev B) to indicate the final As-Built status.

activities.activity_description: Draft the User Manual, detailing safe operational procedures, ergonomic handling limits, and visual cues for correct use.

low skill requirement).

documents_generated: Include specific tolerance limits (e.g.
maximum wear limits before part replacement is required).

page numbers
and IMHOGEN branding are consistent across the package.

activities.activity_description: Route the compiled Final TDP to the Project Manager and Lead Engineer for a final administrative and technical sign-off.

activities.activity_description: Move all finalized native CAD files, FEA simulation data, and internal emails into the project's permanent "Closed/Archived" digital folder.
documents_generated: Apply strict "Read-Only" permissions to the archived folder for all staff except IT Admins.

activities.activity_description: Change the state of all associated CAD files within the PDM software to "Released / Locked" to prevent further parametric editing.
documents_generated: Verify that the PDM system registers the lock and severs check-out privileges for design staff.

documents_generated: Strip all confidential client IP from CAD assemblies before adding them to the general IMHOGEN parts library.

activities.activity_description: Update the central tracking system status to "Documentation Complete" and trigger the Client Relations Team to initiate physical/digital handover.
documents_generated: Confirm the Client Relations Team has acknowledged receipt of the Approved TDP.

activities.activity_description: Receive the "Delivery & Handover Notice" and coordinate a delivery date/time with the client's receiving department.
documents_generated: Confirm the client has the necessary lifting equipment (e.g.
forklift
hoist) available on-site for unloading.

activities.activity_description: Secure an appropriate transport vehicle (e.g., standard pickup or flatbed) based on the structural dimensions and weight.
documents_generated: Verify the vehicle payload capacity exceeds the final As-Built weight of the delivered equipment.

activities.activity_description: Prepare the physical structure for transport by securing any movable clamps, applying protective wrap to precision datums, and loading it onto the transport vehicle.
activities.operator_category: Pallet Jacks, Ratchet Straps, Protective Wrap

activities.activity_description: Print physical copies of the Approved Client TDP (User Manual, Maintenance Guide, QA Certificates) and place them in a weatherproof document pouch attached to the shipment.
documents_generated: Verify all pages are printed clearly and bound professionally before dispatch.

activities.activity_description: Arrive at the client site, supervise the safe unloading of the equipment, and move it to the designated operational footprint (e.g., workshop floor).
documents_generated: Ensure the equipment is placed on a stable
level surface as defined in the initial site requirements.

activities.activity_description: Perform a post-transit visual inspection and basic leveling/alignment using standard hand tools to ensure the structure survived transport intact.
documents_generated: Re-verify the primary datum plane squareness before handing it over to the client operators.

activities.activity_description: Conduct a physical walkthrough with the client's operators, demonstrating how to load a workpiece, engage clamps, and safely operate the equipment.
documents_generated: Force the client operators to physically perform the loading
clamping sequence themselves while you observe.

documents_generated: Finance must issue the final invoice within 24 hours of receiving the handover authorization.
